member.name: T:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.Bindings.RhinoMapperBinding
member.summary: Binding for managing Rhino object mappings to Revit categories.

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.Bindings.RhinoMapperBinding.GetAvailableLayers
member.summary: Gets list of available layers for the UI dropdown.

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.Bindings.RhinoMapperBinding.HighlightObjects(System.String[])
member.summary: Selects/highlights specific objects in Rhino.

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.Bindings.RhinoMapperBinding.AssignObjectsToCategory(System.String[],System.String)
member.summary: Assigns selected objects to a specific Revit category.
member.remarks: If block instance is selected, mapping is applied uniformly to all instances of that block definition in the doc.

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.Bindings.RhinoMapperBinding.ClearObjectsCategoryAssignment(System.String[])
member.summary: Removes category assignments from specific objects.
member.remarks: If block instance is selected, assignment is cleared uniformly from all instances of that block definition in the doc.

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.Bindings.RhinoMapperBinding.ClearAllObjectsCategoryAssignments
member.summary: Removes all category assignments in the doc.

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.Bindings.RhinoMapperBinding.GetCurrentObjectsMappings
member.summary: Gets all current mappings to show in the UI table.

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.Bindings.RhinoMapperBinding.GetCategoryMappingsForObjects(System.String[])
member.summary: Gets category mappings for specific object IDs.

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.Bindings.RhinoMapperBinding.AssignLayerToCategory(System.String[],System.String)
member.summary: Assigns selected layers to a specific Revit category.

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.Bindings.RhinoMapperBinding.ClearLayerCategoryAssignment(System.String[])
member.summary: Removes category assignments from specific layer(s).

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.Bindings.RhinoMapperBinding.ClearAllLayerCategoryAssignments
member.summary: Removes all layer category assignments in the doc.

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.Bindings.RhinoMapperBinding.GetCurrentLayerMappings
member.summary: Gets all current layer mappings to show in the UI table. Layers with the same category mapping are grouped together.

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.Bindings.RhinoMapperBinding.GetCategoryMappingsForLayers(System.String[])
member.summary: Gets category mappings for specific layer IDs.

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.Bindings.RhinoMapperBinding.OnObjectChanged(System.Object,Rhino.DocObjects.RhinoObjectEventArgs)
member.summary: Called when objects are added, deleted, or undeleted in Rhino.

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.Bindings.RhinoMapperBinding.OnObjectAttributesChanged(System.Object,Rhino.DocObjects.RhinoModifyObjectAttributesEventArgs)
member.summary: Called when object attributes are modified in Rhino.
member.remarks: Includes detection for when objects move between layers with mappings.

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.Bindings.RhinoMapperBinding.OnDocumentChanged(System.Object,System.EventArgs)
member.summary: Called when the document changes (e.g., switching to a different Rhino model). Refreshes the mappings table and defined layers to reflect the new document's state.

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.Bindings.RhinoMapperBinding.OnLayerTableEvent(System.Object,Rhino.DocObjects.Tables.LayerTableEventArgs)
member.summary: Called when layer table events occur in Rhino. Refreshes layer list when layer structure changes.
member.remarks: Layer mapping changes are handled by OnObjectAttributesChanged

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.Bindings.RhinoMapperBinding.NotifyMappingsChanged
member.summary: Sends updated mappings to the frontend.

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.Bindings.RhinoMapperBinding.NotifyLayersChanged
member.summary: Sends updated layer list to the frontend.

member.name: P:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.Bindings.RhinoSendBinding.ChangedObjectIds
member.summary:
Used internally to aggregate the changed objects' id. Objects in this list will be reconverted.
Note we're using a concurrent dictionary here as the expiry check method is not thread safe, and this was causing problems. See: [CNX-202: Unhandled Exception Occurred when receiving in Rhino](https://linear.app/speckle/issue/CNX-202/unhandled-exception-occurred-when-receiving-in-rhino) As to why a concurrent dictionary, it's because it's the cheapest/easiest way to do so. https://stackoverflow.com/questions/18922985/concurrent-hashsett-in-net-framework

member.name: P:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.Bindings.RhinoSendBinding.ChangedObjectIdsInGroupsOrLayers
member.summary: Stores objects that have "changed" only the commit structure/proxies - they do not need to be reconverted.

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.Bindings.RhinoSendBinding.RunExpirationChecks
member.summary: Checks if any sender model cards contain any of the changed objects. If so, also updates the changed objects hashset for each model card - this last part is important for on send change detection.

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.Extensions.SpeckleAttributeExtensions.GetAttributes(Speckle.Sdk.Models.Base)
member.summary: Gets Rhino object attributes from a Base, including name and user strings.

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.Extensions.BoundingBoxExtensions.UnionRhinoObjects(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Rhino.DocObjects.RhinoObject})
member.summary: Calculate bounding box with given rhino objects.
member.param: RhinoObjects to calculate union of bounding box.

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.Extensions.SpeckleApplicationIdExtensions.GetSpeckleApplicationId(System.Drawing.Color,Rhino.DocObjects.ObjectColorSource)
member.summary: Retrieves a unique Speckle application id from the rgb value and color source.
member.remarks: Uses the rgb value and source since color names are not unique

member.name: T:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.HostApp.DataObjectInstanceGrouper
member.summary: Groups block instances created from DataObject with InstanceProxies as display values and applies DataObject metadata.

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.HostApp.DataObjectInstanceGrouper.GroupAndApplyProperties
member.summary: After all instances have been created, we then run through the data object instance registry to see which instances belonged to a data object. The method then groups all instances to "re-assemble" the original data object and applies the properties of the data object on to the instances.
member.remarks: This is a deferred action and can only occur once the RhinoInstanceBaker has done its thing.

member.name: T:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.HostApp.Properties.PropertiesExtractor
member.summary: Extracts properties for rhino objects.

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.HostApp.Properties.PropertiesExtractor.ParseArchivableToDictionary(System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary{System.String,System.Object},Rhino.Collections.ArchivableDictionary)
member.summary: Copies an ArchivableDictionary to a Dictionary

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.HostApp.RhinoViewUnpacker.UnpackViews(Rhino.DocObjects.Tables.NamedViewTable)
member.summary: Iterates through a given set of rhino named views to create proxies
member.param: current document named views

member.name: T:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.HostApp.RhinoViewBaker
member.summary: Utility class that creates Named Views during receive.

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.HostApp.RhinoViewBaker.BakeViews(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyCollection{Speckle.Objects.Other.Camera})
member.summary: Bakes Camera objects as Named Views in Rhino.

member.name: T:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.HostApp.RhinoIdleManager
member.summary: Rhino Idle Manager is a helper util to manage deferred actions.

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.HostApp.RhinoIdleManager.#ctor(Speckle.Connectors.DUI.Bridge.IIdleCallManager)
member.summary: Rhino Idle Manager is a helper util to manage deferred actions.

member.name: T:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.HostApp.RhinoLayerHelper
member.summary: Helper class for common Rhino layer and object operations. Consolidates layer utilities to eliminate duplication across the codebase.

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.HostApp.RhinoLayerHelper.GetAvailableLayers
member.summary: Gets list of available layers for UI dropdowns.

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.HostApp.RhinoLayerHelper.GetFullLayerPath(Rhino.DocObjects.Layer)
member.summary: Gets the full layer path with / delimiter

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.HostApp.RhinoLayerHelper.GetLayer(System.String)
member.summary: Converts a string layer ID to a Layer.
member.returns: Layer if found and valid, null otherwise

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.HostApp.RhinoLayerHelper.HasLayerMapping(System.Int32)
member.summary: Helper to check if a layer (by index) has a category mapping.
member.remarks: This is arguably a very specific method pertaining to mapper and maybe shouldn't be in this class?

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.HostApp.RhinoLayerHelper.GetObjectsInLayerHierarchy(Rhino.DocObjects.Layer)
member.summary: Gets all RhinoObjects in the specified layer and all its child layers recursively.

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.HostApp.RhinoLayerHelper.GetLayerAndAllChildren(Rhino.DocObjects.Layer)
member.summary: Gets the specified layer and all its child layers recursively.

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.HostApp.RhinoLayerHelper.GetAllChildLayers(Rhino.DocObjects.Layer)
member.summary: Recursively gets all child layers of the specified parent layer.

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.HostApp.RhinoLayerHelper.IsLayerVisible(System.Int32)
member.summary: Checks if a layer is visible by its index.

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.HostApp.RhinoLayerHelper.FilterByLayerVisibility``1(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0})
member.summary: Filters a collection of objects to only include those on visible layers.

member.name: T:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.HostApp.RhinoObjectHelper
member.summary: Helper class for common Rhino object operations.

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.HostApp.RhinoObjectHelper.GetRhinoObject(System.String)
member.summary: Converts a string object ID to a RhinoObject.
member.returns: RhinoObject if found and valid, null otherwise

member.name: P:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.HostApp.RhinoColorBaker.ObjectColorsIdMap
member.summary: For receive operations

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.HostApp.RhinoColorBaker.ParseColors(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyCollection{Speckle.Sdk.Models.Proxies.ColorProxy})
member.summary: Parse Color Proxies and stores in ObjectColorsIdMap the relationship between object ids and colors

member.name: P:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.HostApp.RhinoColorUnpacker.ColorProxies
member.summary: For send operations

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.HostApp.RhinoColorUnpacker.ProcessObjectColor(System.String,System.Drawing.Color,Rhino.DocObjects.ObjectColorSource,System.Nullable{System.Int32})
member.summary: Processes an object's color and adds the object id to a color proxy in if object color is set ByColor, ByMaterial, or ByParent.

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.HostApp.RhinoColorUnpacker.UnpackColors(System.Collections.Generic.List{Rhino.DocObjects.RhinoObject},System.Collections.Generic.List{Rhino.DocObjects.Layer})
member.summary: Iterates through a given set of rhino objects and layers to collect colors.
member.param: atomic root objects, including instance objects
the layers corresponding to collections on the root collection

member.summary: Bakes in the host app doc instances. Assumes constituent atomic objects already present in the host app.
member.param: Instance definitions and instances that need creating.
A dict mapping { original application id -> [resulting application ids post conversion] }

member.name: T:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.HostApp.RhinoLayerBaker
member.summary: Utility class managing layer creation. Expects to be a scoped dependency per receive operation.

member.name: F:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.HostApp.RhinoLayerBaker._hostLayerCache
member.summary: The layer cache storing the full name of created layers.
member.remarks: The case-agnostic requirement applies to some models (eg Revit with linked models) that may have multiple collections with the same name but with different capitalizations.

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.HostApp.RhinoLayerBaker.CreateBaseLayer(System.String)
member.summary: Creates the base layer and adds it to the cache.

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.HostApp.RhinoLayerBaker.CreateAllLayersForReceive(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Speckle.Sdk.Models.Collections.Collection[]},System.String)
member.summary: Creates all layers needed for receiving data.
member.param: Collections of paths
Name of the base layer
member.remarks: Make sure this is executing on the main thread, using e.g RhinoApp.InvokeAndWait.

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.HostApp.RhinoLayerBaker.GetLayerIndex(Speckle.Sdk.Models.Collections.Collection[],System.String)
member.summary: Retrieves the index of a layer based on the given collection path and base layer name.
member.param: The array containing the collection path to the layer.
The name of the base layer.
member.returns: The index of the layer in the cache.
member.exception: Thrown when the layer is not found in the cache. This can happen if you didn't call previously

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.HostApp.RhinoLayerBaker.CreateLayerFromPath(Speckle.Sdk.Models.Collections.Collection[],System.String)
member.summary: Creates a layer based on the given collection path and adds it to the Rhino document.
member.param: An array of Collection objects representing the path to create the layer.
The base layer name to start creating the new layer.

member.name: T:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.HostApp.RhinoLayerUnpacker
member.summary: Utility class managing layer extraction. Expects to be a scoped dependency per send operation.

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.HostApp.RhinoLayerUnpacker.GetUsedLayers
member.summary: Use this method to get all of the layers that correspond to collection created in the root collection.
member.exception: Throws when a layer could not be retrieved from a stored collection application id

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.HostApp.RhinoLayerUnpacker.GetHostObjectCollection(Rhino.DocObjects.Layer,Speckle.Sdk.Models.Collections.Collection)

summary.para: Use this method to construct the root commit object while converting objects.
Returns the host collection corresponding to the provided layer. If it's the first time that it is being asked for, it will be created and stored in the root object collection.

member.param: The layer you want the equivalent collection for.
The root object that will be sent to Speckle, and will host all collections.

member.name: P:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.HostApp.RhinoMaterialBaker.ObjectIdAndMaterialIdMap
member.summary: A map keeping track of ids, either layer id or object id, and their Render Material Guid. It's generated from the material proxy list as we .

member.name: P:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.HostApp.RhinoMaterialUnpacker.RenderMaterialProxies
member.summary: For send operations

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.HostApp.RhinoMaterialUnpacker.ProcessObjectMaterial(System.String,Rhino.Render.RenderMaterial,Rhino.DocObjects.Material,Rhino.DocObjects.ObjectMaterialSource)
member.summary: Processes an object's material and adds the object id to a material proxy in if object color is set ByObject or ByParent.

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.HostApp.RhinoMaterialUnpacker.UnpackRenderMaterials(System.Collections.Generic.List{Rhino.DocObjects.RhinoObject},System.Collections.Generic.List{Rhino.DocObjects.Layer})
member.summary: Iterates through a given set of rhino objects and layers to collect render materials.
member.param: atomic root objects, including instance objects
the layers corresponding to collections on the root collection

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.HostApp.SpeckleRhinoPanelHost.Reinitialize(Speckle.Connectors.DUI.WebView.DUI3ControlWebView)
member.summary: This is a lot like PanelsOnClosed but called when trying to show the panel to clear out a lingering parent as PanelsOnClosed isn't called

member.name: T:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.Mapper.Revit.LayerCategoryMapping
member.summary: Represents layers that are all assigned to the same category.

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.Mapper.Revit.LayerCategoryMapping.#ctor(System.String,System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{System.String},System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{System.String},System.Int32)
member.summary: Represents layers that are all assigned to the same category.

member.name: T:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.Mapper.Revit.LayerOption
member.summary: Represents a layer option for the UI dropdown.

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.Mapper.Revit.LayerOption.#ctor(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Represents a layer option for the UI dropdown.

member.name: T:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.Mapper.Revit.CategoryMapping
member.summary: Represents a group of objects that are all assigned to the same category.

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.Mapper.Revit.CategoryMapping.#ctor(System.String,System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{System.String},System.Int32)
member.summary: Represents a group of objects that are all assigned to the same category.

member.name: T:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.Mapper.Revit.RevitMappingConstants
member.summary: Shared constants for the Rhino-to-Revit mapping system. Centralizes string constant(s) to avoid duplication across three using classes.

member.name: T:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.Mapper.Revit.RevitMappingResolver
member.summary: Responsible for resolving category mappings from layer hierarchy. Used by the send pipeline to resolve mappings during property extraction.
member.remarks: This gets called when no mapping found on the object level.

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.Mapper.Revit.RevitMappingResolver.GetEffectiveObjectsForLayerMapping(System.String[],System.String)
member.summary: Gets all objects that would effectively receive the specified layer mapping during send. Takes into account hierarchical resolution - only returns objects that would actually resolve to this specific category value through the layer hierarchy.

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.Mapper.Revit.RevitMappingResolver.SearchLayerHierarchyForMapping(Rhino.DocObjects.RhinoObject)
member.summary: Traverses layer hierarchy, returns first mapping found or null

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.Mapper.Revit.RevitMappingResolver.GetLayerByIndex(System.Int32)
member.summary: Gets a layer by its index from the active doc.

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.Mapper.Revit.RevitMappingResolver.GetParentLayer(Rhino.DocObjects.Layer)
member.summary: Gets the parent layer of the given layer.

member.name: T:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.Operations.Send.RhinoContinuousTraversalBuilder
member.summary: NOTE: I am not happy this is a mostly copy paste of the Root object builder, but i'm also not too worried. The main (hot) path should be this one going forward, so we should not touch the og root object builder besides to delete it. Stateless builder object to turn an into a object

member.name: T:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.Operations.Send.RhinoRootObjectBuilder
member.summary: Stateless builder object to turn an into a object

member.name: T:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.Operations.Receive.DisableRedrawScope
member.summary: Helper class to disable within a scope

summary.para: Expects to be a scoped dependency per receive operation.

member.name: M:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.Operations.Receive.RhinoHostObjectBuilder.BakeObject(Rhino.Geometry.GeometryBase,Speckle.Sdk.Models.Base,System.String,Rhino.DocObjects.ObjectAttributes)
member.summary: Bakes an object to the document.
member.param: Parent object ID for color and material proxies search (if fallback conversion was used)
member.remarks: Material and Color attributes are processed here due to those properties existing sometimes on fallback geometry (instead of parent). and this method is called by

member.name: P:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.Plugin.SpeckleConnectorsRhinoCommand.Instance
member.summary: The only instance of this command.

member.name: P:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.Plugin.SpeckleConnectorsRhinoCommand.EnglishName
member.returns: The command name as it appears on the Rhino command line.

summary.para: Every RhinoCommon .rhp assembly must have one and only one PlugIn-derived class. DO NOT create instances of this class yourself. It is the responsibility of Rhino to create an instance of this class.

To complete plug-in information, please also see all PlugInDescription attributes in AssemblyInfo.cs (you might need to click "Project" -> "Show All Files" to see it in the "Solution Explorer" window).

member.name: P:Speckle.Connectors.Rhino.Plugin.SpeckleConnectorsRhinoPlugin.Instance
member.summary: Gets the only instance of the Speckle_Connectors_Rhino7Plugin plug-in.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.AllowNullAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that null is allowed as an input even if the corresponding type disallows it.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.ConstantExpectedAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the specified method parameter expects a constant.
member.remarks: This can be used to inform tooling that a constant should be used as an argument for the annotated parameter.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.ConstantExpectedAttribute.Min
member.summary: Indicates the minimum bound of the expected constant, inclusive.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.ConstantExpectedAttribute.Max
member.summary: Indicates the maximum bound of the expected constant, inclusive.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DisallowNullAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that null is disallowed as an input even if the corresponding type allows it.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DoesNotReturnAttribute
member.summary: Applied to a method that will never return under any circumstance.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DoesNotReturnIfAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that the method will not return if the associated Boolean parameter is passed the specified value.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DoesNotReturnIfAttribute.#ctor(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Initializes the attribute with the specified parameter value.
member.param: The condition parameter value. Code after the method will be considered unreachable by diagnostics if the argument to the associated parameter matches this value.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DoesNotReturnIfAttribute.ParameterValue
member.summary: Gets the condition parameter value.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.ExperimentalAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that an API is experimental and it may change in the future.
member.remarks: This attribute allows call sites to be flagged with a diagnostic that indicates that an experimental feature is used. Authors can use this attribute to ship preview features in their assemblies.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.ExperimentalAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class, specifying the ID that the compiler will use when reporting a use of the API the attribute applies to.
member.param: The ID that the compiler will use when reporting a use of the API the attribute applies to.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.ExperimentalAttribute.DiagnosticId
member.summary: Gets the ID that the compiler will use when reporting a use of the API the attribute applies to.
member.remarks:
The diagnostic ID is shown in build output for warnings and errors.
This property represents the unique ID that can be used to suppress the warnings or errors, if needed.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.ExperimentalAttribute.UrlFormat
member.summary: Gets or sets the URL for corresponding documentation. The API accepts a format string instead of an actual URL, creating a generic URL that includes the diagnostic ID.
member.value: The format string that represents a URL to corresponding documentation.
member.remarks: An example format string is https://contoso.com/obsoletion-warnings/{0}.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MaybeNullAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that an output may be null even if the corresponding type disallows it.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MaybeNullWhenAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that when a method returns , the parameter may be null even if the corresponding type disallows it.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MaybeNullWhenAttribute.#ctor(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Initializes the attribute with the specified return value condition.
member.param: The return value condition. If the method returns this value, the associated parameter may be null.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MaybeNullWhenAttribute.ReturnValue
member.summary: Gets the return value condition.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MemberNotNullAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that the method or property will ensure that the listed field and property members have not-null values.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MemberNotNullAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes the attribute with a field or property member.
member.param: The field or property member that is promised to be not-null.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MemberNotNullAttribute.#ctor(System.String[])
member.summary: Initializes the attribute with the list of field and property members.
member.param: The list of field and property members that are promised to be not-null.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MemberNotNullAttribute.Members
member.summary: Gets field or property member names.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MemberNotNullWhenAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that the method or property will ensure that the listed field and property members have not-null values when returning with the specified return value condition.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MemberNotNullWhenAttribute.#ctor(System.Boolean,System.String)
member.summary: Initializes the attribute with the specified return value condition and a field or property member.
member.param: The return value condition. If the method returns this value, the associated parameter will not be null.
The field or property member that is promised to be not-null.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MemberNotNullWhenAttribute.#ctor(System.Boolean,System.String[])
member.summary: Initializes the attribute with the specified return value condition and list of field and property members.
member.param: The return value condition. If the method returns this value, the associated parameter will not be null.
The list of field and property members that are promised to be not-null.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MemberNotNullWhenAttribute.ReturnValue
member.summary: Gets the return value condition.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MemberNotNullWhenAttribute.Members
member.summary: Gets field or property member names.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.NotNullAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that an output will not be null even if the corresponding type allows it. Specifies that an input argument was not null when the call returns.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.NotNullIfNotNullAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that the output will be non-null if the named parameter is non-null.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.NotNullIfNotNullAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes the attribute with the associated parameter name.
member.param: The associated parameter name. The output will be non-null if the argument to the parameter specified is non-null.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.NotNullIfNotNullAttribute.ParameterName
member.summary: Gets the associated parameter name.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.NotNullWhenAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that when a method returns , the parameter will not be null even if the corresponding type allows it.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.NotNullWhenAttribute.#ctor(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Initializes the attribute with the specified return value condition.
member.param: The return value condition. If the method returns this value, the associated parameter will not be null.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.NotNullWhenAttribute.ReturnValue
member.summary: Gets the return value condition.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.SetsRequiredMembersAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that this constructor sets all required members for the current type, and callers do not need to set any required members themselves.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute
member.summary: Specifies the syntax used in a string.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes the with the identifier of the syntax used.
member.param: The syntax identifier.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.#ctor(System.String,System.Object[])
member.summary: Initializes the with the identifier of the syntax used.
member.param: The syntax identifier.
Optional arguments associated with the specific syntax employed.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.Syntax
member.summary: Gets the identifier of the syntax used.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.Arguments
member.summary: Optional arguments associated with the specific syntax employed.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.CompositeFormat
member.summary: The syntax identifier for strings containing composite formats for string formatting.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.DateOnlyFormat
member.summary: The syntax identifier for strings containing date format specifiers.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.DateTimeFormat
member.summary: The syntax identifier for strings containing date and time format specifiers.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.EnumFormat
member.summary: The syntax identifier for strings containing format specifiers.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.GuidFormat
member.summary: The syntax identifier for strings containing format specifiers.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.Json
member.summary: The syntax identifier for strings containing JavaScript Object Notation (JSON).

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.NumericFormat
member.summary: The syntax identifier for strings containing numeric format specifiers.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.Regex
member.summary: The syntax identifier for strings containing regular expressions.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.TimeOnlyFormat
member.summary: The syntax identifier for strings containing time format specifiers.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.TimeSpanFormat
member.summary: The syntax identifier for strings containing format specifiers.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.Uri
member.summary: The syntax identifier for strings containing URIs.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.Xml
member.summary: The syntax identifier for strings containing XML.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnscopedRefAttribute
member.summary: Used to indicate a byref escapes and is not scoped.

remarks.para: There are several cases where the C# compiler treats a as implicitly - where the compiler does not allow the to escape the method.

For example:
for instance methods.
parameters that refer to types.
parameters.
This attribute is used in those instances where the should be allowed to escape.

Applying this attribute, in any form, has impact on consumers of the applicable API. It is necessary for API authors to understand the lifetime implications of applying this attribute and how it may impact their users.

member.name: T:System.Index
member.summary: Represent a type can be used to index a collection either from the start or the end.

member.name: M:System.Index.#ctor(System.Int32,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Construct an Index using a value and indicating if the index is from the start or from the end.
member.param: The index value. it has to be zero or positive number.
Indicating if the index is from the start or from the end.
member.remarks: If the Index constructed from the end, index value 1 means pointing at the last element and index value 0 means pointing at beyond last element.

member.name: P:System.Index.Start
member.summary: Create an Index pointing at first element.

member.name: P:System.Index.End
member.summary: Create an Index pointing at beyond last element.

member.name: M:System.Index.FromStart(System.Int32)
member.summary: Create an Index from the start at the position indicated by the value.
member.param: The index value from the start.

member.name: M:System.Index.FromEnd(System.Int32)
member.summary: Create an Index from the end at the position indicated by the value.
member.param: The index value from the end.

member.name: P:System.Index.Value
member.summary: Returns the index value.

member.name: P:System.Index.IsFromEnd
member.summary: Indicates whether the index is from the start or the end.

member.name: M:System.Index.GetOffset(System.Int32)
member.summary: Calculate the offset from the start using the giving collection length.
member.param: The length of the collection that the Index will be used with. length has to be a positive value
member.remarks: For performance reason, we don't validate the input length parameter and the returned offset value against negative values. we don't validate either the returned offset is greater than the input length. It is expected Index will be used with collections which always have non negative length/count. If the returned offset is negative and then used to index a collection will get out of range exception which will be same affect as the validation.

member.name: M:System.Index.Equals(System.Object)
member.summary: Indicates whether the current Index object is equal to another object of the same type.
member.param: An object to compare with this object

member.name: M:System.Index.Equals(System.Index)
member.summary: Indicates whether the current Index object is equal to another Index object.
member.param: An object to compare with this object

member.name: M:System.Index.GetHashCode
member.summary: Returns the hash code for this instance.

member.name: M:System.Index.op_Implicit(System.Int32)~System.Index
member.summary: Converts integer number to an Index.

member.name: M:System.Index.ToString
member.summary: Converts the value of the current Index object to its equivalent string representation.

member.name: T:System.Range
member.summary: Represent a range has start and end indexes.

member.name: P:System.Range.Start
member.summary: Represent the inclusive start index of the Range.

member.name: P:System.Range.End
member.summary: Represent the exclusive end index of the Range.

member.name: M:System.Range.#ctor(System.Index,System.Index)
member.summary: Construct a Range object using the start and end indexes.
member.param: Represent the inclusive start index of the range.
Represent the exclusive end index of the range.

member.name: M:System.Range.Equals(System.Object)
member.summary: Indicates whether the current Range object is equal to another object of the same type.
member.param: An object to compare with this object

member.name: M:System.Range.Equals(System.Range)
member.summary: Indicates whether the current Range object is equal to another Range object.
member.param: An object to compare with this object

member.name: M:System.Range.GetHashCode
member.summary: Returns the hash code for this instance.

member.name: M:System.Range.ToString
member.summary: Converts the value of the current Range object to its equivalent string representation.

member.name: M:System.Range.StartAt(System.Index)
member.summary: Create a Range object starting from start index to the end of the collection.

member.name: M:System.Range.EndAt(System.Index)
member.summary: Create a Range object starting from first element in the collection to the end Index.

member.name: P:System.Range.All
member.summary: Create a Range object starting from first element to the end.

member.name: M:System.Range.GetOffsetAndLength(System.Int32)
member.summary: Calculate the start offset and length of range object using a collection length.
member.param: The length of the collection that the range will be used with. length has to be a positive value.
member.remarks: For performance reason, we don't validate the input length parameter against negative values. It is expected Range will be used with collections which always have non negative length/count. We validate the range is inside the length scope though.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.CallerArgumentExpressionAttribute
member.summary: An attribute that allows parameters to receive the expression of other parameters.

member.name: M:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.CallerArgumentExpressionAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The condition parameter value.

member.name: P:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.CallerArgumentExpressionAttribute.ParameterName
member.summary: Gets the parameter name the expression is retrieved from.

member.name: M:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.CollectionBuilderAttribute.#ctor(System.Type,System.String)
member.summary: Initialize the attribute to refer to the method on the type.
member.param: The type of the builder to use to construct the collection.
The name of the method on the builder to use to construct the collection.
member.remarks: must refer to a static method that accepts a single parameter of type and returns an instance of the collection being built containing a copy of the data from that span. In future releases of .NET, additional patterns may be supported.

member.name: P:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.CollectionBuilderAttribute.BuilderType
member.summary: Gets the type of the builder to use to construct the collection.

member.name: P:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.CollectionBuilderAttribute.MethodName
member.summary: Gets the name of the method on the builder to use to construct the collection.
member.remarks: This should match the metadata name of the target method. For example, this might be ".ctor" if targeting the type's constructor.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.CompilerFeatureRequiredAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that compiler support for a particular feature is required for the location where this attribute is applied.

member.name: M:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.CompilerFeatureRequiredAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Creates a new instance of the type.
member.param: The name of the feature to indicate.

member.name: P:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.CompilerFeatureRequiredAttribute.FeatureName
member.summary: The name of the compiler feature.

member.name: P:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.CompilerFeatureRequiredAttribute.IsOptional
member.summary: If true, the compiler can choose to allow access to the location where this attribute is applied if it does not understand .

member.name: F:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.CompilerFeatureRequiredAttribute.RefStructs
member.summary: The used for the ref structs C# feature.

member.name: F:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.CompilerFeatureRequiredAttribute.RequiredMembers
member.summary: The used for the required members C# feature.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.InterpolatedStringHandlerArgumentAttribute
member.summary: Indicates which arguments to a method involving an interpolated string handler should be passed to that handler.

member.name: M:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.InterpolatedStringHandlerArgumentAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The name of the argument that should be passed to the handler.
member.remarks: may be used as the name of the receiver in an instance method.

member.name: M:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.InterpolatedStringHandlerArgumentAttribute.#ctor(System.String[])
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The names of the arguments that should be passed to the handler.
member.remarks: may be used as the name of the receiver in an instance method.

member.name: P:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.InterpolatedStringHandlerArgumentAttribute.Arguments
member.summary: Gets the names of the arguments that should be passed to the handler.
member.remarks: may be used as the name of the receiver in an instance method.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.InterpolatedStringHandlerAttribute
member.summary: Indicates the attributed type is to be used as an interpolated string handler.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.IsExternalInit
member.summary: Reserved to be used by the compiler for tracking metadata. This class should not be used by developers in source code.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.ModuleInitializerAttribute
member.summary: Used to indicate to the compiler that a method should be called in its containing module's initializer.
member.remarks:
When one or more valid methods with this attribute are found in a compilation, the compiler will emit a module initializer which calls each of the attributed methods.
Certain requirements are imposed on any method targeted with this attribute: - The method must be `static`. - The method must be an ordinary member method, as opposed to a property accessor, constructor, local function, etc. - The method must be parameterless. - The method must return `void`. - The method must not be generic or be contained in a generic type. - The method's effective accessibility must be `internal` or `public`.
The specification for module initializers in the .NET runtime can be found here: https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/blob/main/docs/design/specs/Ecma-335-Augments.md#module-initializer

member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.OverloadResolutionPriorityAttribute
member.summary: Specifies the priority of a member in overload resolution. When unspecified, the default priority is 0.

member.name: M:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.OverloadResolutionPriorityAttribute.#ctor(System.Int32)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The priority of the attributed member. Higher numbers are prioritized, lower numbers are deprioritized. 0 is the default if no attribute is present.

member.name: P:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.OverloadResolutionPriorityAttribute.Priority
member.summary: The priority of the member.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.ParamCollectionAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that a method will allow a variable number of arguments in its invocation.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.RequiredMemberAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that a type has required members or that a member is required.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.RequiresLocationAttribute
member.summary: Reserved for use by a compiler for tracking metadata. This attribute should not be used by developers in source code.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.SkipLocalsInitAttribute
member.summary: Used to indicate to the compiler that the .locals init flag should not be set in method headers.

member.name: M:System.Runtime.Versioning.RequiresPreviewFeaturesAttribute.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: M:System.Runtime.Versioning.RequiresPreviewFeaturesAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified message.
member.param: An optional message associated with this attribute instance.

member.name: P:System.Runtime.Versioning.RequiresPreviewFeaturesAttribute.Message
member.summary: Returns the optional message associated with this attribute instance.

member.name: P:System.Runtime.Versioning.RequiresPreviewFeaturesAttribute.Url
member.summary: Returns the optional URL associated with this attribute instance.
